create modern web applications that combine performance, aesthetics, and intuitive usability. I focus on high-quality code, attention to detail, and solutions that address real user and business needs. 
 Feel free to explore my professional experience, projects, and the technologies I work with on a daily basis.

muddyCodeWorkspace.description:
Work in an Nx monorepo within a large-scale ERP multi-application system. The project involved development and maintenance of multiple business modules as well as shared components and services.

Scope of work in business modules:

• Sales module:
Contractor dictionaries, customer orders, price lists, and services.

• PMS:
Technological and production logic, release versions, job records, calculations (features, feature relations, quotation register), extensive dictionaries (tools, tool types, operations, workstations, workstation rates, qualifications, modifiers).

• Simulations:
Simulation versions and simulation records.

• WMS:
Index groups.

• LOGS:
System logs module (monitoring and displaying application logs).

• MES:
Production monitoring and control system – production process handling, employee work logging, production order tracking, material consumption records, and real-time operation status tracking.

Services and system architecture

• QBE Service:
Building and handling Query By Example (QBE) queries.

• Attachment Service:
File upload and download with progress tracking (XMLHttpRequest with progress events), backend integration, and streaming downloads on the frontend side.

• User Settings – managing user settings stored in localStorage:
- table and view configurations
- language settings
- access control

• Authentication and session mechanisms
- login and session recovery mechanism
- token expiration handling and request retry after refresh
- request cancellation on user change
- HTTP interceptors responsible for token and authentication handling
- idle timeout mechanism based on user activity (mouse event tracking)

Application and access management

• ActiveApp Service:
Control of available applications and features per user (permission system).

• Components and UI layer
Shared components:
- loader
- attachment component
- error handling system based on snackbar service
- extended tree component (tree view, dynamic node add/remove, hover detail preview, process path generation and visualization)
- development and support of components:
- modal system
- numeric keyboard component

For over 1.5 years of work on the project, I was responsible for the development of an ERP system – on the frontend layer, system architecture, and shared components. The work included expanding existing modules, implementing new features, and stabilizing and evolving the system in an enterprise environment.

muddyCodeSite.description: The “Muddy Code” website was built using the Wix platform. It is a simple website containing information about the company, its services, and a contact section enabling direct communication with clients.

familiaMedSite.description: Development and maintenance of the project, SEO work (search engine optimization), analysis and fixing of existing bugs, and refactoring of legacy parts of the system. Expansion of the homepage and subpages (including Familiamed Clinique – eyelid surgery), as well as improving performance and stability of the website. Continued development of the project after previous teams, with a focus on improving code quality and user experience.

quickAtest.description: The single-page application “Quick Atest” was designed to streamline the process of creating and merging documents required for order execution. It enables content filtering, generating data into Excel files, and downloading selected documents as a single merged PDF file.

muddyCode.description: Since January 2024, I have been working as a frontend developer, responsible for building and maintaining web application interfaces. I focus on improving usability, performance, and visual consistency of products. Over time, I moved from simpler tasks to working on more complex components and system modules. I actively collaborate with backend and UX/UI teams, participating in designing and implementing new features.

montService.description: I was responsible for diagnosing, repairing, and maintaining devices used in sales systems, such as cash registers and payment terminals. I performed installation, configuration, and troubleshooting of hardware and electronic components. I ensured the reliability of devices and quick restoration of their functionality, guaranteeing uninterrupted operation of point-of-sale systems.

aboutMePage.text: I am a Frontend Developer with 3 years of experience in creating modern and functional web applications. I combine programming skills with a sense of aesthetics and attention to detail, which allows me to build solutions that are both efficient and user-friendly. I quickly learn new technologies and enjoy taking on new challenges. Programming and IT in a broad sense are my passion, which is why I continuously develop my skills and am also interested in game development and new technology trends. Outside of work, I actively practice sports, which teaches me consistency, discipline, and perseverance in achieving goals.
